source_bullets.text: Locating on a map the major Indigenous nations of what would eventually become Canada at different points in the early eighteenth century (see, e.g., A3.1)
Using maps to show territorial claims or territorial changes over time (see, e.g., A3.3, A3.5)
Using historical maps to examine Indigenous land use and settlement patterns (see, e.g., A3.2)
Locating on a map the communities mentioned in the study of this period (see, e.g., B3.1, B3.2, B3.3)
Using maps to show the spread and impacts of colonialism (see, e.g., B1.1)
